title: Oru Megham Lyrics Ishtam Songs Lyrics
stanzas:
Singer(s): MLR Karthikeyan, Divya
Song: Oru Megham
Movie: Ishtam
Music by : Thaman S

Naan indru naanai illai, yen endru ketkiren
Nee indri pona naatkal, veen endru solkiren (2)

Oru megham illaamal
oru saaral illaamal
muludhaaga dhegam ninaikinrene naane
Vizhudhaaga kaatril aadum vazhkai dhaane

Oru vaarthai illaamal
oru osai illaamal
oru paadal ondru paaadi vandhen naane
Idhu paadhi sorgam, paadhi naragam thaane

Poi solli katrukonden
naakoosa villaye
Mun pola unnaal indru
naan pesa villaye (2)

Oru vaarthai illaamal
oru osai illaamal
oru paadal ondru paaadi vandhen naane
Idhu paadhi sorgam, paadhi naragam thaane

Oru thaeneeril naan kaanaadha
suvai un peril unardhene
Unnai paarthaale en melaadai
sari seidhene adhanaale enna maayamo

Porukku povadhu pol thoongida selkiren
Peruku kannai moodi verodho seikiren

Aalillaa theevukulle naan indru pogiren
Aaanaalum kaadhil yedho sangeedham ketkiren

Oru vaarthai illaamal
oru osai illaamal
oru paadal ondru paaadi vandhen naane
Idhu paadhi sorgam, paadhi naragam thaane

Vazhi theriyaadha mozhi puriyaadha
oru siruvan pol nadandhene
Ada idhuthaano idhu adhu dhaano
enna adayaalam theriyaamal naanum nindrene

Thevaiku mele ingae thaen thuli sottudhe
Kai theendum dhooram vandhu vanamdhan muttudhe
Noorodu nootronrai ennaatkal ponadhe
Verodu maari indru veredho aagudhe

enrichedMetadata.faq:
Frequently Asked Questions

What is the meaning of the song 'Oru Megham'?

The song 'Oru Megham' conveys feelings of longing and emotional connection, reflecting on love and memories.

What is the context of the song in the movie 'Ishtam'?

In the movie 'Ishtam', the song plays during a pivotal moment that highlights the protagonists' emotional journey and their relationship.

Who are the singers of 'Oru Megham'?

The song is sung by Divya and MLR Karthikeyan, whose voices blend beautifully to create a romantic atmosphere.

Who composed the music for 'Oru Megham'?

The music for 'Oru Megham' was composed by Thaman S, known for his captivating melodies.

When was 'Oru Megham' released?

'Oru Megham' was released on April 27, 2012.
enrichedMetadata.summary: In 'Oru Megham', the protagonists express their deep emotions and longing through a heartfelt duet. The song encapsulates the essence of love, portraying the bittersweet moments of reminiscence. With soothing melodies and poignant lyrics, it evokes a sense of nostalgia and warmth. The collaboration between Divya and MLR Karthikeyan, under Thaman S's direction, creates a memorable musical experience that resonates with listeners.
enrichedMetadata.high_ctr_intro: Oru Megham from the movie Ishtam beautifully captures the essence of longing and love. With the enchanting voices of Divya and MLR Karthikeyan, this duet is a soothing melody that resonates with the heart. Thaman S's musical composition elevates the emotional depth, making it a must-listen for romantic souls.
stanzaMeanings: I am not myself today, I ask why. The days without you, I say they are lost.
Without a cloud, without a drizzle, I feel my body completely. Life dances in the wind.
Without a word, without a sound, I have come singing a song. This is half heaven, half hell.
I have learned to lie, I cannot speak like a fool today.
Without a word, without a sound, I have come singing a song. This is half heaven, half hell.
In a sweet water, I cannot see the taste, I remember your name. Seeing you, I have made my song right, what a wonder that is.
Like something that is lost, I will sleep. I close my eyes and wonder what is happening.
Today, I am going into the depths of the sea. But still, I hear some music in my heart.
Without a word, without a sound, I have come singing a song. This is half heaven, half hell.
Like a child who does not know the way, I walk without understanding the words. Is this it or that, I do not know and I stand still.
Here, the honey drops fall on the need. The distance comes close and kisses me. With a hundred new things, today is different from yesterday.
Dheemthana naagerey dheem dheem thana naagerey
This is half heaven, half hell.
